n8n-setup.title: Complete n8n setup guide - free forever

posts.content: Thread: How to set up n8n for free on Oracle Cloud. 24GB RAM, always free. Let's go!
Step 1: Create Oracle Cloud account at cloud.oracle.com. Select Always Free tier. You get 4 OCPUs, 24GB RAM.
Step 2: Create Ubuntu 22.04 ARM instance. Download SSH key. Connect via terminal.
Step 3: Install Docker. Run: curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sh
Step 4: Create docker-compose.yml with n8n + PostgreSQL. Copy from my GitHub.
Step 5: Run docker-compose up -d. Access at http://your-ip:5678
Done! You now have n8n running 24/7 for $0. Full script in my GitHub repo. DM for link.
Thread: Docker crash course. Everything you need to know in 10 posts.
What is Docker? It packages your app with all dependencies into a container. Runs anywhere the same way.
Key concept: Dockerfile. It's a recipe for building your image. FROM, RUN, COPY, CMD are the basics.
Key concept: docker-compose.yml. Defines multiple containers. Perfect for app + database setups.
Command: docker build -t myapp . - builds your image from Dockerfile.
Command: docker run -p 3000:3000 myapp - runs your container, maps ports.
Command: docker-compose up -d - starts all services defined in compose file.
Pro tip: Use .dockerignore to exclude node_modules, .git, etc from your image.
Pro tip: Multi-stage builds reduce image size dramatically. Build in one stage, copy only what's needed.
That's it! You now know Docker basics. Practice with my templates on GitHub. Link in profile.

github-actions.title: GitHub Actions CI/CD from zero to hero

posts.content: Thread: GitHub Actions CI/CD guide. Automate testing, building, deploying.
What are GitHub Actions? Free CI/CD built into GitHub. Runs workflows on events (push, PR, schedule).
Basic workflow structure: name, on (trigger), jobs, steps. YAML format in .github/workflows/
Example: Test on every PR. Use ubuntu-latest, checkout code, install deps, run tests.
Example: Deploy on push to main. Build Docker image, push to registry, deploy to server.
Secrets: Store API keys in Settings > Secrets. Reference as ${{ secrets.KEY_NAME }}
Pro tip: Use matrix strategy to test across multiple Node/Python versions simultaneously.
Pro tip: Cache dependencies with actions/cache to speed up workflows by 50%.
Pro tip: Use workflow_dispatch for manual triggers. Perfect for deployments.
You're now ready to automate everything! Full workflow templates in my GitHub repo.
